sutta: monks there are these three persons found existing in the world what three the topsy-turvy brain the scatter brain and the man of comprehensive brain of what sort monks is the topsy-turvy brain herein a certain person frequents the monastery to hear dhamma from the lips of the monks the monks teach him dhamma that is lovely in the beginning lovely in the middle lovely in the end both in spirit and in letter they make plain the holy life perfectly fulfilled in all its purity but as he sits there he pays no heed to that talk in its beginning pays no heat to its middle pays no he to its end also when he has risen from his seat he pays no heat there too just as when a pot is turned upside down the water poured thereon runs off and does not stay in the pot even so in this case a certain person frequents the monastery but pays no heed to that talk also when he rises from his his seat he pays no heat there too this one is called the topsy-turvy brain and of what sword monks is the scatter brain in this case a certain person frequents the monastery as he sits he pays heed to that talk in its beginning its middle and its end but when he has risen up from his seat he pays no heat there too just as when in a man's lap diverse kinds of food are piled together such as sesame rice sweet meats and jujube fruits when he rises from his seed he scatters all abroad through absent-mindedness even so amongst in this case a certain person frequents the monastery but when he has risen from his seat he pays no heed there too this one is called a scatter brain now what's what monks is the man of comprehensive mind in this case a certain person frequents the monastery to hear dhamma from the lips of the monks they teach him dhamma that is lovely in the beginning lovely in the middle lovely in the end both in its spirit and its letter they make plain the holy life perfectly fulfilled in all its purity as he sits there he pays heed to that talk in its beginning he pays he to that tongue in his middle he pays heed to its ending also when he rises from his seat he still bears it in mind just as when a pot is set upright the water poured therein accumulates and does not run away even so in this case a person frequents the monastery and pays heed to that talk also when he rises from his seat he bears it in in mind in its beginning in its middle and in its ending this one monks is called the man of comprehensive mind such monks are the three persons found existing in the world
commentary: i think this suta is quite plain there you can understand the topsy turvy brain person he doesn't pay attention his mind is so scattered now when he's sitting there listening to the talk he cannot concentrate on the talk then when he gets up after he leaves the monastery also he's forgotten what the talk was all about the second one he pays attention when the talk is is going on but when he goes back he does not reflect on that on the top it's all by going in one ear and gone out the other is forgotten about what was taught instead of reflecting on it the third one is he appears attention to it when the talk is delivered and even after the talk he goes back he still reflects it in his mind and he remembers what was heard so you all can examine yourselves and see which category you fall into now
